section_or_object: Death Star turbolaser / gun tower family
notes: Auction records from 2016 and 2024 confirm real biscuit-foam gun towers with separate turret tops; Paul Huston identifies the Death Star cannon as the key model in ILM's first completed shot.

section_or_object: equatorial trench backwall / hangar-bay light strip
what_seems_to_exist: clear trench backwall with rectangular light openings
notes: The trench appears to have been a physically distinct clear backwall/light system rather than just painted paneling; the current archive state seems altered.
The crane is real enough to appear in local specialist threads and an official StarWars.com fan feature describing it as glimpsed briefly from a single angle in one shot during the trench run.

what_seems_to_exist: silo-like surface structures distinct from gun towers
notes: The local tower thread argues these are their own special structures, built from a different logic than the turbolaser towers.
Local gantry research says the original was built by Jon Erland and that the reconstruction is based on a behind-the-scenes image showing the miniature being built.

section_or_object: Death Star hangar / magnetic-shield bay miniature section
what_seems_to_exist: a miniature hangar accent section associated with the gantry
notes: The gantry evidence implies a larger Death Star docking-bay miniature context, likely including the magnetic-shield bay area seen with the Falcon.

what_seems_to_exist: possible hanging ceiling rig or accent element in the hangar miniature
notes: The local gantry thread mentions a replica Dykstraflex hanging above on the hangar ceiling, implying an original ceiling-hung accent or rig.

what_seems_to_exist: likely a real named Death Star structure, miniature not yet visually locked
notes: The dialogue reference clearly belongs to ANH, and local specialists keep circling it, but the current evidence set does not yet securely match a physical miniature object.

section_or_object: large radar dish or other dish-like Death Star accessory
notes: Not yet strong enough to state as a closed row; may be a real special section or a memory-collision with other Death Star details.
No convincing current evidence that the torpedo-hit area survived or was built as a separately identifiable special section beyond the trench/backwall system.
